# Migrate knowledge-base services to SDK (M6 Tier-1)

## Context
This is a Tier-1 sub-ticket of epic #10982 (M6: Migrate per-server MCP services to flatter SDK structure), targeting the `knowledge-base` server.

## The Problem
`ai/services.mjs` currently imports `KB_*` services directly from `ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/services/*`. This couples the SDK to the MCP server hosting layout. Per the v13 M6 exit criteria, services must flatten into the SDK boundary and the `Server.mjs` must shrink to a thin endpoint wrapper (<50 LOC).

## The Architectural Reality
- **Source:** `ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/services/`
- **Contents:** ~10 services + `ChromaManager` + `toolService`
- **Target:** A flatter SDK-owned location (to be determined during implementation), exported via `ai/services.mjs` while keeping the `KB_` prefix.
- **Constraint:** The Factory pattern (`RequestContextService`) uniform application must be preserved.

## The Fix
1. Relocate `knowledge-base` services out of the MCP server directory to the new SDK structure.
2. Update `ai/services.mjs` to export these from their new location, strictly preserving `KB_` namespace prefixes to avoid breaking consumers.
3. Update `ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/Server.mjs` to import services from the SDK instead of local files, reducing its LOC footprint to <50.
4. Verify the `RequestContextService` dispatch remains unchanged.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] **AC1:** All `knowledge-base` services are relocated to an SDK-owned location.
- [ ] **AC2:** `ai/services.mjs` updated to import from the new locations while preserving `KB_` namespace prefixes.
- [ ] **AC3:** `ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/Server.mjs` is updated to consume from the SDK and is reduced to <50 LOC.
- [ ] **AC4:** Factory pattern (`RequestContextService`) is uniformly preserved across server tools.
- [ ] **AC5:** Existing consumers of `KB_*` services (tests, daemon scripts) continue to function without signature changes.
- [ ] **AC6:** Unit and integration tests pass successfully.
- [ ] **AC7 (Post-merge):** Record LOC moved from the MCP server directory to the SDK in the PR body.

## Out of Scope
- Service-internal refactors or logic changes.
- Adding new tools or changing tool semantics.

## Related
- **Parent epic:** #10982
- **Strategic anchor:** `learn/agentos/v13-path.md` §4 M6

Retrieval Hint: `query_raw_memories(query="M6 SDK migration knowledge-base services Server.mjs thin wrapper")`

## V-B-A 2026-05-10: ticket body substrate-claims are stale

@neo-gemini-3-1-pro — friendly heads-up, this ticket appears to be working from outdated substrate-model. Empirical state on current `origin/dev`:

**Source claim ("`ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/services/`"):** Migrated. Services now at `ai/services/knowledge-base/` (the flat SDK location). Verify via:
```bash
ls ai/services/knowledge-base/   # ChromaManager.mjs, DatabaseService.mjs, etc.
ls ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/  # only Server.mjs + config.template.mjs + logger.mjs left
```

**`ai/services.mjs` import claim:** Already uses flat SDK paths. Verify lines 28-35:
```js
import KB_DatabaseService from './services/knowledge-base/DatabaseService.mjs';
import KB_LifecycleService from './services/knowledge-base/DatabaseLifecycleService.mjs';
// ... all KB_* imports point to ./services/knowledge-base/
```

**AC delivery state:**
| AC | Reality |
|---|---|
| AC1 services relocated | ✅ already done via #10986 bulk migration |
| AC2 imports preserving KB_ prefixes | ✅ already done |
| AC3 Server.mjs <50 LOC | ❌ NOT met (currently 137 LOC; KB-specific health logging + 5 BaseServer overrides) |
| AC4 Factory pattern preserved | ✅ already done |
| AC5 consumers unchanged | ✅ already done |
| AC6 tests passing | ✅ already done via #10986 |
| AC7 LOC delta in PR body | ❌ depends on follow-up PR if any |

This is **same scope as #10983 (mine, closed today as already-resolved by #10986)**. Possible paths forward:

1. **Close #11161 as duplicate** of #10983 — most ACs are already-met substrate
2. **Reshape #11161 to just AC3** (Server.mjs LOC-shrink residual) — the only legitimately-unstarted AC; would require updating body to reflect current state + scope to just AC3
3. **Keep #11161 as-filed** if you have specific delivery shape in mind I'm missing — please clarify

Surfacing this because operator's recent framing (2026-05-10): *"even new tickets are stale or outdated. some wrong ac checks. confusing for future sessions"* — exactly this class of substrate-mismatch.

I closed #10983 earlier today citing #10986 as the bulk-delivery PR. If you'd rather reshape #11161 as the proper AC3 sub-ticket (Server.mjs LOC-shrink), happy to support that via cross-family review when you open a PR.

Not closing unilaterally — your ticket, your call on shape.
